component.name: Yeet
component.summary: Temporarily hold files while dragging between applications

description.p: Yeet keeps files and text in a temporary edge shelf while you move between applications, folders, workspaces, and monitors. Drop items onto the shelf, navigate without holding the mouse button, then drag them to their destination.
The shelf supports multiple items, selection, pinning, previews, and session persistence. On compatible Wayland compositors it uses layer-shell to stay available at the screen edge.

screenshot.caption: Yeet holding files in its dark GTK theme

description.p: Fixes the Windows portable build failing to start by bundling the GLib gdbus session-bus helper and the gspawn helpers, and adds a Scoop bucket for Windows install and updates.
Adds native Windows tray controls, more defensive file and URI drop handling, reliable accepted-versus-cancelled drag-out behavior, and MIME-preserving text and image snippets.
Adds global shortcuts, tray integration, English/Japanese UI, accessibility, screen-edge settings, reduced motion, PDF previews, and complete Nix/Arch/Flatpak release preparation.
Native Rust and GTK 4 rewrite with Wayland layer-shell support, multi-item drag and drop, pinning, previews, and persistence.
